title: Pending Corrections
description: Spec-silent shape (the approved build plan's Phase 2 section names 'data/pending_corrections.json' -- fed by audit.yml's findings or manual owner edits, drained first by the analyst each run -- without fixing fields; decision logged in IMPROVEMENT_BACKLOG.md). Top-level object: a version plus a queue of correction candidates awaiting the analyst's evidence check. A confirmed pending item becomes a content/corrections.json entry (schemas/corrections.schema.json) and its card's status is set to 'corrected'; an entry here is removed once actioned (not schema-tracked -- reconcile_run.py's own job). Phase 2 seeds data/pending_corrections.json = {"version": 1, "pending": []}. Phase 6 adds optional target_type/target_id, mirroring schemas/corrections.schema.json's own addition, so a pending candidate can eventually name a content/companies/<id>.json profile as well as a card. Phase 9 fixes a real end-to-end gap Phase 6 left open and Phase 9's own auditor checks were the first real writer to hit: card_id was unconditionally required even for a target_type:'company' entry, which has no card to name at all -- forcing a fabricated card_id (a violation of this project's own no-fabrication rule) just to pass schema validation. card_id is now required only when target_type is absent or 'card' (an 'if target_type==company then {} else {required:[card_id]}' conditional below), preserving every existing card-only entry's shape exactly (no target_type -> card_id still required, unchanged) while letting a genuine company-targeted candidate omit it. See IMPROVEMENT_BACKLOG.md for the discovery and fix, logged in full.

card_id.description: content/cards/<id>.json this candidate correction targets. Required whenever target_type is absent or 'card' (this schema's own if/target_type==company/then/else conditional, Phase 9) -- i.e. for every one of today's card-only entries, unchanged. Omitted (and must be omitted, never fabricated) for a target_type:'company' entry, which has no card to name -- see target_type/target_id below.

target_type.description: Optional: what kind of record target_id names. Must be present together with target_id or not at all (dependentRequired below). Phase 9's auditor/profile_staleness.py and auditor/linkrot.py::audit_company_hijacked_links, wired through auditor/corrections_feed.py, are the first real writers of target_type:'company' entries; target_type:'card' (or omitted) remains the analyst's own card-only shape, unchanged.

target_id.description: Optional (Phase 6, forward-looking): the id of the record this candidate correction targets -- a content/cards/<id>.json id when target_type is 'card', or a content/companies/<id>.json id when target_type is 'company'. Must be present together with target_type or not at all.

issue_description.description: What might be wrong with the card, in plain language.

evidence_url.description: A source the analyst should fetch and weigh when checking this candidate.

source.description: Whether audit.yml's own checks (e.g. link rot, missed-story) or a manual owner edit raised this candidate.
